title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: baseballr R package acquiring analyzing baseball data online sources. wraps MLB Stats API, ESPN (MLB), FanGraphs, Baseball Reference, Baseball Savant / Statcast, NCAA baseball stats site, Spotrac, Chadwick Bureau register, Retrosheet, exporting ~290 functions across multiple data-source families: mlb_*(), espn_mlb_*(), fg_*(), bref_*(), statcast_*() / scrape_statcast_savant*(), ncaa_*(), sptrc_*(), chadwick_*(), retrosheet_*(), plus load_*() data loaders, metrics/woba_plus/fip_plus analytics, ggspraychart() visualization. guide differs current repository docs, treat CONTRIBUTING.md current test implementations authoritative. Version: 2.0.0 R Requirement: >= 4.1.0 (package uses native pipe |>) License: MIT Author (aut): Bill Petti billpetti@gmail.com Maintainer (cre): Saiem Gilani saiem.gilani@gmail.com pkgdown site: https://billpetti.github.io/baseballr/ Repo: BillPetti/baseballr Branch: master default branch release branch.

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: master default branch release branch (main). Active development happens development_branch; create feature branches latest stable base target master PRs. Keep code, tests, roxygen/docs updates PR changing exported behavior.

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: Use native pipe |> everywhere. magrittr (%>%) retained dependency NCAA wrappers still use – introduce new %>% usage non-NCAA code, prefer migrating NCAA code |> opportunistically.

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: make_baseballr_data() (R/utils.R) coerces tibble, sets class c("baseballr_data", "tbl_df", "tbl", "data.table", "data.frame"), attaches baseballr_timestamp baseballr_type attributes.
code: raw_data |>   data.frame(stringsAsFactors = FALSE) |>   dplyr::as_tibble() |>   janitor::clean_names() |>   make_baseballr_data("Description from source", Sys.time())

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: Every wrapper returns variable assigned inside tryCatch must initialize variable (usually <- NULL) tryCatch block. Otherwise, upstream site errors (500s, timeouts, connection resets, IP bans) error handler runs, return variable never bound, return(<var>) throws object '<var>' found instead intended cli::cli_alert_danger() + empty fallback. rule applies every return variable name – , df, pbp, leaders, payrolls, roster, resp, data, etc. Initialize appropriate empty value: NULL single-object return, data.frame() / list() documented shape.
code: mlb_func <- function(...) {   url <- "https://statsapi.mlb.com/..."    out <- NULL    # <-- MANDATORY. Not inside tryCatch.    tryCatch(     expr = {       resp <- request_with_proxy(url, ...)       out  <- resp |>         # ...parse... |>         janitor::clean_names() |>         make_baseballr_data("Data from MLB.com", Sys.time())     },     error = function(e) {       cli::cli_alert_danger("{Sys.time()}: Invalid arguments or no data available!")       cli::cli_alert_danger("Error: {conditionMessage(e)}")     },     warning = function(w) {       cli::cli_alert_warning("{Sys.time()}: Warning: {conditionMessage(w)}")     },     finally = {}   )   return(out) }

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: user-facing messages use cli: - cli::cli_alert_danger() error handlers - cli::cli_alert_warning() warnings - cli::cli_alert_info() informational notes - cli::cli_warn() / cli::cli_abort() raised conditions Never pass raw condition object cli_* function – message string glue-interpolated, condition object render correctly (can leak glue syntax message). Pass conditionMessage(cond) value placeholder instead:
code: # WRONG error = function(e) cli::cli_alert_danger("Error: {e}")  # RIGHT error = function(e) cli::cli_alert_danger("Error: {conditionMessage(e)}")

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: upstream sites (MLB Stats API, FanGraphs, Baseball Reference, Savant) add, rename, occasionally drop columns time. dropping known-transient column, use dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of("colname")) instead dplyr::select(-"colname"). bare form errors moment upstream removes column; any_of -ops silently. any_of guard applies renames: dplyr::rename(dplyr::any_of(c(new = "old"))).

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: Use %||% null-safe defaults parsing variable-shaped JSON: data.frame(list_of_items) can produce unexpected column types API returns mixed NULL/value fields – supply %||% defaults binding.

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: baseballr talks several different hosts (statsapi.mlb.com, fangraphs.com, baseball-reference.com, baseballsavant.mlb.com, NCAA stats site, spotrac.com, ESPN’s site.api / sports.core.api / site.web.api hosts), single base-URL proxy convention single-source packages. Requests go package’s helpers R/utils.R, R/utils_mlb_stats.R, R/utils_espn.R (mlb_api_call(), fg_api_call(), request_with_proxy(), .retry_request(), csv_from_url(), rds_from_url()); build wrapper helper appropriate source rather calling httr/httr2 directly. ESPN MLB wrappers (espn_mlb_*()) live R/espn_mlb_*.R (public) shared internals R/espn_baseball_*_helpers.R box parsers R/espn_mlb_box_helpers.R. share httr2 layer R/utils_espn.R (.retry_request(), .resp_text(), .report_api_error() / .report_api_warning(), .empty_baseballr_data()). .retry_request() honours proxy options(baseballr.proxy = ...) (ESPN wrappers thread ..., per-call proxy override). Use .report_api_error(e, hint, args) ESPN error handlers (.args <- mget(setdiff(names(formals()), "..."))), raw cli::cli_alert_danger(). ESPN’s baseball game-summary boxscore groups stats side (batting/pitching/fielding), box parsers baseball-specific rather shared basketball siblings. FanGraphs requires Cloudflare-exempt User-Agent. 2026-06-03 FanGraphs sits behind Cloudflare JS challenge 403s (cf-mitigated: challenge) every unrecognized client – including plain/library User-Agent used slip – header/TLS tweak passes (needs JS runtime). challenge exempts okhttp client FanGraphs mobile app uses, FanGraphs requests go fg_api_call(), sends User-Agent: okhttp/4.12.0 (emits cli hint 403 still slips ). mlb_api_call() keeps plain package User-Agent MLB Stats API (ignores ). .aspx scrapers (fg_guts(), fg_park(), fg_park_hand()) FanGraphs game-log functions send okhttp UA inline. Build new FanGraphs wrappers fg_api_call(), never mlb_api_call(). (okhttp-UA approach adapted upstream PR #405; see NEWS.md.)

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: Tests live tests/testthat/. Many hit live sites gated / skipped (e.g. skip_on_cran(), skip_on_ci()); assume run unconditionally. IMPORTANT CAUTION – NCAA IP bans. NCAA stats site aggressively IP-bans scrapers. Tests dev work hit NCAA endpoints (ncaa_*(), load_ncaa_baseball_*()) must done sparingly cached – prefer running reusing fixtures, never tight loop, keep skipped CI.

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: Every test hits external service gated behind source-specific helper tests/testthat/helper-skip.R. helper skip()s unless environment variable set "1", R CMD check (CI) never fails upstream site , rate-limiting, changed schema, blocking automated access. first line every live test_that() block source gate. Set NCAA_BASEBALL_TESTS=1 deliberately sparingly (NCAA site IP-bans scrapers). Purely computational tests (e.g. metrics_* supplied data frame) gated.
code: # Run one source's live tests locally: Sys.setenv(MLB_STATS_TESTS = "1"); devtools::test()

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: Always use subset direction column assertions. upstream sites add columns, strict expect_equal break new column. rule : expected list must subset actual columns. Anti-patterns avoid: dynamic columns, expect_true((core_cols %% colnames(x))) equivalent subset-direction expect_in(). Per-element null checks: function returns named list frames source sometimes returns fewer expected, use inline helper individual asserts skip gracefully rather erroring:
code: test_that("mlb_function returns expected columns", {   skip_mlb_test()          # source gate -- always the first line   skip_on_cran()    x <- mlb_function(game_pk = 632970)    # Skip-if-empty guard -- right after the API call, before any column asserts.   if (is.null(x) || !is.data.frame(x) || nrow(x) == 0) {     skip("No rows returned from endpoint at test time")   }    cols <- c("col1", "col2", "col3")   expect_in(sort(cols), sort(colnames(x)))   # expected ⊆ actual   expect_s3_class(x, "data.frame") }) # WRONG - flags when upstream adds a column, even though it's non-breaking expect_equal(sort(colnames(x)), sort(cols))  # WRONG - same direction problem, just phrased with expect_in expect_in(sort(colnames(x)), sort(cols)) check_cols <- function(component, cols) {   if (is.null(component) || !is.data.frame(component) || ncol(component) == 0) {     return(invisible(NULL))   }   expect_in(sort(cols), sort(colnames(component)))   expect_s3_class(component, "data.frame") }

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: regulation game 9 innings; extra innings resolve ties. Tests parsers must assume fixed inning count. MLB game identifiers game_pk values (e.g. 632970), used throughout mlb_*() family. MLB Stats API base host https://statsapi.mlb.com/. Player identity cross-source: Chadwick Bureau register (chadwick_player_lu(), playerid_lookup(), playername_lookup()) maps MLBAM, FanGraphs, Baseball Reference, Retrosheet IDs – prefer joining data across families. V2/V3 dichotomy NBA/WNBA stats packages; source native payload shape.

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: Three files describe release different audiences. Whenever add NEWS.md bullet, think three committing: NEWS.md – authoritative changelog downstream users; rendered pkgdown changelog. New bullets go recent development version heading; create new version section ahead release. cran-comments.md – gets submitted CRAN. Every behavioral user-visible change NEWS.md reflected submission. Purely internal changes (refactors, test infrastructure, dev tooling) can omitted. _pkgdown.yml – pkgdown reference index. New exported functions need land right reference: section. baseballr uses starts_with() selectors (e.g. starts_with("mlb_"), starts_with("fg_"), starts_with("bref")) new functions matching prefixes picked automatically; explicitly-listed functions need manual entry. Preview pkgdown::build_site() doubt. change touches API surface (new export, deprecation, removal), include one-line note commit message confirming ’ve checked three.

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: Use Conventional Commits: Prefer scoped commit subjects useful (e.g. feat(mlb): ..., docs(instructions): ...). Use type!: BREAKING CHANGE: footer breaking changes. Split unrelated work separate commits reviewability. Important: Never include AI agents assistants (e.g. Claude, Copilot) co-authors commits. Omit Co-Authored-trailers referencing AI tools.
code: feat: add mlb_game_context_metrics() endpoint wrapper fix: initialize return value before tryCatch in fg_team_pitcher() docs: update NEWS.md for the next dev release test: add fg_batter_leaders column validation refactor: migrate ncaa_roster() from %>% to native pipe chore: update .Rbuildignore patterns ci: bump actions/checkout to v5

title: CLAUDE.md – baseballr Development Guide
text: Always initialize return variable (<- NULL, etc.) tryCatch block – see Return-Value Initialization . Never pass raw condition object cli_* function; pass conditionMessage(cond) value placeholder. NCAA stats site IP-bans scrapers – run ncaa_*() work sparingly cache results; keep tests skipped CI. Use native pipe |>; add new %>% usage outside legacy NCAA wrappers still depend magrittr. Drop known-transient columns dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(...)) schema drift upstream survivable. Upstream sites add columns time – use subset-direction assertions tests. data.frame(list_of_items) can yield wrong column types source returns mixed NULL/value fields – supply %||% defaults first. Never hand-edit NAMESPACE files man/; regenerate devtools::document(). Local dev artifacts (e.g. .vscode, .claude) can surface R CMD check notes – keep .Rbuildignore current.

title: Contributing to baseballr
text: authoritative reference CLAUDE.md. Key rules: Native pipe |> everywhere. magrittr (%>%) retained dependency NCAA wrappers still use ; introduce new %>% usage. Return-value initialization (CRITICAL). Every wrapper returns variable assigned inside tryCatch must initialize variable (usually <- NULL) tryCatch. Otherwise API error runs error handler, return variable never bound, return(<var>) throws object '<var>' found instead returning empty value message. Messaging via cli. Use cli::cli_alert_danger() error handlers, cli::cli_alert_warning() cli::cli_alert_info() warnings/info, cli::cli_warn() / cli::cli_abort() raised conditions. Never pass raw condition object cli_* call (message glue-interpolated); pass conditionMessage(cond) value placeholder. Column-drift resilience. Upstream sites add occasionally rename columns. dropping known-transient column, use dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of("colname")) rather bare form schema change survivable.

title: Contributing to baseballr
text: Tests live tests/testthat/. Many hit live sites gated / skipped run routine R CMD check CI.

title: Contributing to baseballr
text: Live-API tests gated. Network-dependent tests skip CRAN CI guard empty/transient responses. assume network available check run. NCAA stats site aggressively IP-bans scrapers. Tests development work hit NCAA endpoints (ncaa_*) must done sparingly cached wherever possible. run NCAA tests tight loop, add tests hammer NCAA endpoints. single careless test run can get IP (CI runner’s IP) banned. iterating NCAA wrappers, save sample payload locally develop cached fixture.

title: Contributing to baseballr
text: Always use subset direction column assertions. upstream sources add columns time, strict expect_equal full column set break moment non-breaking column added. rule : expected column list must subset actual columns. Anti-patterns avoid: list-returning endpoints sometimes return fewer elements expected, use inline helper individual asserts skip gracefully rather erroring:
code: test_that("mlb_function returns expected columns", {   skip_on_cran()   skip_on_ci()    x <- mlb_function(game_pk = 632970)    # Skip-if-empty guard -- always right after the API call, before any   # assertion that touches the data. Handles transient errors and outages.   if (is.null(x) || !is.data.frame(x) || nrow(x) == 0) {     skip("No rows returned from endpoint at test time")   }    cols_x <- c("col1", "col2")   expect_true(all(cols_x %in% colnames(x)))   # expected subset of actual   expect_s3_class(x, "data.frame") }) # WRONG -- flags when upstream adds a column, even though it is non-breaking expect_equal(sort(colnames(x)), sort(cols_x))  # WRONG -- same direction problem, just phrased with expect_in expect_in(sort(colnames(x)), sort(cols_x)) check_cols <- function(obj, cols) {   if (is.null(obj) || !is.data.frame(obj) || ncol(obj) == 0) return(invisible(NULL))   expect_true(all(cols %in% colnames(obj)))   expect_s3_class(obj, "data.frame") }

title: Getting Started with baseballr
text: generally speaking nine men’s baseball data sources accessible package: baseballr-data repo (@SportsDataverse) MLB Stats API ESPN (MLB) Baseball Savant’s Statcast Chadwick Bureau’s Public Register Baseball Players Baseball Reference FanGraphs Retrosheet NCAA

what: Function names indicate the data source
title: Getting Started with baseballr
text: baseballr v1.0.0, function naming convention implemented data source indicator appear start function name: Functions use baseballr-data repository contain load_ update_ function name considered loading functions play--play data, team box scores, player box scores. Functions use MLB Stats API start mlb_ convention assumed get functions. baseballr version 2.0.0, package exports ~88 functions covering MLB Stats API. Functions use ESPN’s MLB endpoints start espn_mlb_ convention assumed get functions. cover scoreboards schedules, play--play, team player box scores, game rosters, standings, athletes, coaches, drafts, leaders, — including baseball-specific extractors probable starting pitchers (espn_mlb_game_probables()) umpire crew / venue (espn_mlb_game_info()). baseballr version 2.0.0, package exports ~107 functions covering ESPN’s MLB endpoints. returns clean, tidy baseballr_data tibble; box scores returned wide (one row per team / per athlete-side). Live ESPN tests gated behind ESPN_MLB_TESTS=1. Functions use one Baseball Savant’s Statcast APIs start statcast_ convention assumed get functions. functions allow live access Statcast data MLB games -progress. baseballr version 2.0.0, package exports ~5 Statcast-related functions. Functions use Chadwick Bureau’s Public Register Baseball Players start chadwick_, playerid_, playername_ convention assumed get functions. functions allow access Bureau’s public register baseball players. baseballr version 2.0.0, package exports 3 functions sourced using Chadwick Bureau’s public register baseball players. Functions use Baseball Reference’s website start bref_ convention assumed get functions. baseballr version 2.0.0, package exports ~4 functions covering Baseball Reference. Functions use FanGraphs’s baseball website start fg_ convention assumed get functions. baseballr version 2.0.0, package exports ~11 functions covering FanGraphs.com. Functions use Retrosheet’s baseball data start retrosheet_ convention assumed get functions. baseballr version 2.0.0, package exports 1 function Retrosheet Data. Functions use NCAA website start ncaa_ convention assumed get functions. baseballr version 2.0.0, package exports ~8 function covering NCAA Stats portal.

title: Plotting Statcast data
code: betts_id <- playerid_lookup("Betts") %>%   dplyr::filter(first_name == "Mookie") %>%   dplyr::select(mlbam_id, first_name, last_name)

what: scrape Betts’ Statcast data, by pitch removing those with a batted ball speed of 0
title: Plotting Statcast data
code: betts_15 <- statcast_search("2015-03-31", "2015-10-31", playerid = betts_id[1,1], player_type = 'batter') betts_16 <- statcast_search("2016-03-31", "2016-10-31", playerid = betts_id[1,1], player_type = 'batter')    betts <- dplyr::bind_rows(betts_15, betts_16) %>%   dplyr::mutate(Year = as.factor(substr(game_date,1,4))) %>%   dplyr::filter(type == "X") %>%   dplyr::filter(launch_speed != 0)

what: calculate average launch angles and batted ball speeds by game
title: Plotting Statcast data
code: betts_grpd <- betts %>%   dplyr::group_by(game_date) %>%   dplyr::summarise(     `Average Launch Angle` = mean(launch_angle, na.rm = TRUE),      `Average Batted Ball Speed` = mean(launch_speed, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%   dplyr::ungroup() %>%   melt(id=c("game_date")) %>%   dplyr::mutate(Year = as.factor(substr(game_date,1,4)))

what: calculate Betts’ average launch angle and batted ball speed by year
title: Plotting Statcast data
code: betts_avg_speed_yr <- betts %>%   dplyr::group_by(Year) %>%   dplyr::summarise(     speed = round(mean(launch_speed, na.rm = TRUE),1),      angle = round(mean(launch_angle, na.rm = TRUE),1))

title: Plotting Statcast data
code:
betts_grpd %>%   ggplot(aes(game_date, value)) +   geom_point() +   stat_smooth(aes(group = Year, color = Year)) +   facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free_y") +    ggtitle("
Mookie Betts: 2015 vs. 2016
") +    labs(subtitle = paste0("Betts has lowered his launch angle in 2016, from ", betts_avg_speed_yr[1,3], " degrees in 2015 to ", betts_avg_speed_yr[2,3], " degrees this year.

"),         caption = "@BillPetti
Data from baseballsavant.mlb.com
Data acquired with the baseballr package") +   ylab("Angle = Degrees, Speed = MPH
") +   xlab("
Date") +   #theme_bp_grey() +    theme(legend.position = "bottom", strip.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14), plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust=-.12)) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("#5F9ED1", "#FF800E"))

what: Use Burnes’ ID To Load Statcast Data
title: Using Statcast Pitch Data
text: block download Corbin Burnes’ pitches March 1st December 1st.

title: Using Statcast Pitch Data
text: first time looking Statcast data recommend looking documentation dataset returned. ’ll walk data represented column give better idea data points collected pitch. common data points used pitching analysis: pitcher : Pitcher’s MLBAM ID pitch_name/pitch_type : Pitch Name/Type release_speed : Velocity pfx_z/pfx_x : Pitch Movement release_spin_rate : Spin Rate spin_axis : Spin Axis release_pos_z/release_pos_z/extension : Release Point plate_z/plate_x : Pitch Location Since ’re going making scatterplot Corbin Burnes’ pitch movement, need make sure data proper format match traditional movement plot. pfx_x pfx_z columns feet let’s create two new columns convert inches. pfx_x also catcher’s point view let’s also reverse pitcher’s.
code: # The glimpse function is something I use regularly # to quickly preview the data I'm working with. # Try it out if you haven't used it before! #  #  # burnes_data %>% dplyr::glimpse()  burnes_cleaned_data <- burnes_data %>%    # Only keep rows with pitch movement readings   # and during the regular season   dplyr::filter(!is.na(pfx_x), !is.na(pfx_z),                 game_type == "R") %>%    dplyr::mutate(pfx_x_in_pv = -12*pfx_x,                 pfx_z_in = 12*pfx_z)

title: Using Statcast Pitch Data
text: Now ’ve created new columns, let’s use plot Corbin Burnes’ pitches move.  like use pitch_name color scatterplots gives full pitch name legend, pitch_type also work prefer shorter abbreviations (ex: 4-Seam Fastball = FF). use pitch_type instead, sure make new vector colors.
code: # Make a named vector to scale pitch colors with pitch_colors <- c("4-Seam Fastball" = "red",                   "2-Seam Fastball" = "blue",                   "Sinker" = "cyan",                   "Cutter" = "violet",                   "Fastball" = "black",                   "Curveball" = "green",                   "Knuckle Curve" = "pink",                   "Slider" = "orange",                   "Changeup" = "gray50",                   "Split-Finger" = "beige",                   "Knuckleball" = "gold")  # Find unique pitch types to not have unnecessary pitches in legend burnes_pitch_types <- unique(burnes_cleaned_data$pitch_name)  burnes_cleaned_data %>%    ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(x = pfx_x_in_pv, y = pfx_z_in, color = pitch_name)) +   ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +   ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0) +   # Make the points slightly transparent   ggplot2::geom_point(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.25) +   # Scale the pitch colors to match what we defined above   # and limit it to only the pitches Burnes throws   ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = pitch_colors,                               limits = burnes_pitch_types) +   # Scale axes and add " to end of labels to denote inches   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-25,25),                               breaks = seq(-20,20, 5),                               labels = scales::number_format(suffix = "\"")) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-25,25),                               breaks = seq(-20,20, 5),                               labels = scales::number_format(suffix = "\"")) +   ggplot2::coord_equal() +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Corbin Burnes Pitch Movement",                 subtitle = "2021 MLB Season | Pitcher's POV",                 caption = "Data: Baseball Savant via baseballr",                  x = "Horizontal Break",                 y = "Induced Vertical Break",                 color = "Pitch Name")

title: Using Statcast Pitch Data
text: Now let’s take look Corbin Burnes’ pitch velo inning trademark cutter.
code: burnes_velo_by_inning <- burnes_cleaned_data %>%    dplyr::filter(pitch_name == "Cutter") %>%    dplyr::group_by(inning, pitch_name) %>%    dplyr::summarize(average_velo = mean(release_speed, na.rm = TRUE)) #> `summarise()` has regrouped the output. #> ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by inning and pitch_name. #> ℹ Output is grouped by inning. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(inning, pitch_name))` for per-operation #>   grouping (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.  burnes_velo_by_inning %>%    ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(x = inning, y = average_velo, color = pitch_name)) +   ggplot2::geom_line(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +   ggplot2::geom_point(size = 3, show.legend = FALSE) +   ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = pitch_colors) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:9) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(90, 100)) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Corbin Burnes Cutter Velo By Inning",                 subtitle = "2021 MLB Season",                 caption = "Data: Baseball Savant via baseballr",                 x = "Inning",                 y = "Average Velo") #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning #> was generated.

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions – baseballr
text: Use prefix matches data source. Never mix sources behind one prefix.

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions – baseballr
text: Native pipe. Use |>, %>%. magrittr retained dependency NCAA wrappers still use %>%; add new %>% usage. Return-value initialization (critical). wrapper returns variable assigned inside tryCatch() MUST initialize variable (usually <- NULL) tryCatch block. Otherwise API error leaves variable unbound return() throws object '<var>' found instead failing gracefully. Also guard post-tryCatch processing (e.g. (!.null(x)) colnames(x) <- ...). Messaging via cli. Use cli::cli_alert_danger() error handlers, cli::cli_alert_warning() / cli::cli_alert_info() elsewhere, cli::cli_warn() / cli::cli_abort() raised conditions. Never pass raw condition object cli::cli_*() – text glue-interpolated, message containing { error. Pass conditionMessage(cond) via value placeholder: cli::cli_alert_danger("{conditionMessage(cond)}"). Data pipeline. Finish transforms ... |> janitor::clean_names() |> make_baseballr_data("<description>", Sys.time()). make_baseballr_data() sets class c("baseballr_data", "tbl_df", "tbl", "data.table", "data.frame") attaches baseballr_timestamp / baseballr_type attributes. Column-drift resilience. Drop columns dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of("col")), never bare select(-"col"), upstream sources add remove columns time. Null-safe defaults. Use %||% fallbacks. Default arguments. Give single default value (e.g. output = "default"), document choices, validate inside function. use match.arg-style c(...) choice-vector defaults signatures. HTTP helpers / FanGraphs. Don’t call httr/httr2 directly; use source helpers (mlb_api_call(), fg_api_call(), request_with_proxy(), .retry_request()). FanGraphs sits behind Cloudflare 403s plain/library User-Agents, route FanGraphs requests fg_api_call() – sends Cloudflare-exempt okhttp/4.12.0 UA. mlb_api_call() keeps plain UA MLB Stats API. (okhttp-UA approach adapted upstream PR #405.) ESPN MLB (espn_mlb_*). Public wrappers R/espn_mlb_*.R thin shims R/espn_baseball_*_helpers.R; game-summary box parsers live R/espn_mlb_box_helpers.R. share httr2 layer R/utils_espn.R (.retry_request(), honouring options(baseballr.proxy = ...)) report via .report_api_error() / .report_api_warning(), raw cli::cli_alert_*().
code: my_fn <- function(...) {   df <- NULL                      # initialize BEFORE tryCatch   tryCatch(     expr = { df <- ... |> make_baseballr_data("desc", Sys.time()) },     error = function(e) cli::cli_alert_danger("{conditionMessage(e)}")   )   return(df) }

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions – baseballr
text: Tests live tests/testthat/. Many hit live endpoints gated/skipped (skip_on_cran(), skip_on_ci(), source-specific guards). Assert columns subset direction: expected names must subset actual names (expect_in(sort(expected), sort(colnames(x)))), new upstream column break test. NCAA caution: NCAA stats site aggressively IP-bans scrapers. add tests run dev code hammers NCAA endpoints; cache fixtures instead.

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions – baseballr
text: man/ NAMESPACE roxygen2-generated – never hand-edit ; run devtools::document(). New exported functions must picked _pkgdown.yml (family sections use starts_with() selectors). changing exported behavior, update NEWS.md keep cran-comments.md sync user-visible changes.

title: GitHub Copilot Instructions – baseballr
text: Use Conventional Commits (feat:, fix:, docs:, test:, refactor:, chore:). Never add AI tools commit co-authors. Omit Co-Authored-trailer references AI assistant.

title: Acquiring and Analyzing Baseball Data

text: package consists two main sets functions: data acquisition metric calculation. example, want see standings specific MLB division given date, can use bref_standings_on_date() function. Just pass year, month, day, division want: Right now function works far back 1994, leagues split three divisions. can also pull data hitters specific date range. results hitters August 1st October 3rd 2015 season: terms metric calculation, package allows user calculate consistency team scoring run prevention year using team_consistency(): can also calculate wOBA per plate appearance wOBA contact set data date range, provided data available. Simply pass proper data frame woba_plus: can also generate wOBA-based stats, well FIP, pitchers using fip_plus() function:

what: Scrape Pitcher Performance Data Over a Custom Time Frame — bref_daily_pitcher
title: Scrape Pitcher Performance Data Over a Custom Time Frame — bref_daily_pitcher
text: function allows scrape basic pitcher statistics custom time frame. Data sourced Baseball-Reference.com.

title: Scrape Pitcher Performance Data Over a Custom Time Frame — bref_daily_pitcher
Scrape Pitcher Performance Data Over a Custom Time Frame — bref_daily_pitcher
text: t1 First date data scraped . take form "YEAR-MONTH-DAY" t2 Last date data scraped . take form "YEAR-MONTH-DAY"

title: Scrape Pitcher Performance Data Over a Custom Time Frame — bref_daily_pitcher
text: Returns tibble pitcher performance requested date range, one row per player, following columns:

what: Detect a stats.ncaa.org Akamai interstitial challenge — .ncaa_is_interstitial
title: Detect a stats.ncaa.org Akamai interstitial challenge — .ncaa_is_interstitial
text: stats.ncaa.org routes (e.g. /teams/{id}/season_to_date_stats) gated behind Akamai Bot Manager. challenged, server returns HTTP 200 body short interstitial shell (bm-verify meta-refresh, akamai_validation.html iframe, request_quota_reached.html notice) rather data page. static request solve challenge, detect shell degrade gracefully instead silently scraping page data tables.

title: ESPN MLB Endpoint Overview — espn_mlb
text: Wrappers around ESPN's Major League Baseball endpoints (espn_mlb_*()). cover ESPN's three public hosts: site.api.espn.com – scoreboard, schedule, game summary (play--play, team / player box scores), teams, rosters. sports.core.api.espn.com – reference data: athletes, coaches, seasons, franchises, draft, futures, leaders, standings, events, plays, per-competitor game detail. site.web.api.espn.com – athlete overviews, game logs, splits, league-wide statistics. complement MLB Stats API wrappers (mlb_*()), FanGraphs (fg_*()), Baseball Reference (bref_*()), Baseball Savant / Statcast (statcast_*()) families. espn_mlb_*() naming mirrors sister SportsDataverse packages – hoopR (espn_nba_* / espn_mbb_*), wehoop (espn_wnba_* / espn_wbb_*), cfbfastR (espn_cfb_*).

title: ESPN MLB Endpoint Overview — espn_mlb
text: espn_mlb_pbp() – play--play (pitch- -bat-level). espn_mlb_scoreboard() – scores / schedule date (YYYYMMDD). espn_mlb_team_box() – team batting / pitching / fielding box score. espn_mlb_player_box() – player batting / pitching box score. espn_mlb_game_all() – three (plays, team box, player box) one call. espn_mlb_game_rosters() – two game-day rosters.

title: ESPN MLB Endpoint Overview — espn_mlb
text: espn_mlb_teams(), espn_mlb_team_roster(), espn_mlb_standings(). espn_mlb_team_stats(), espn_mlb_player_stats(), espn_mlb_leaders(). espn_mlb_athletes_index(), espn_mlb_player_info(), espn_mlb_draft().

title: ESPN MLB Endpoint Overview — espn_mlb
text: ESPN wrappers call .retry_request() directly without ..., per-call proxy overrides supported. Route requests options(baseballr.proxy = ...) http_proxy / https_proxy environment variables instead.

what: Get ESPN MLB Athlete Career Stats (Long Format) — espn_mlb_player_career_stats
title: Get ESPN MLB Athlete Career Stats (Long Format) — espn_mlb_player_career_stats
text: Returns career stats MLB athlete long format. Default stat_type = 0L fetches standard "Splits" / regular-season view. Pass vector like c(0L, 1L, 2L) attempt multiple types bind via stat_type_id column; variants 404 athlete silently skipped. Stat types: 0 = regular season (default endpoint), 1 = postseason, 2 = career aggregate. Coverage types 1 2 sparse — many athletes type 0 populated.

title: Get ESPN MLB Athlete Career Stats (Long Format) — espn_mlb_player_career_stats
Get ESPN MLB Athlete Career Stats (Long Format) — espn_mlb_player_career_stats
text: athlete_id ESPN athlete identifier. stat_type Integer integer vector stat-type codes. Default 0L fetches standard "Splits" / regular-season view. Pass vector like c(0L, 1L, 2L) bind multiple types via stat_type_id column; non-existent variants silently skipped. ... Additional arguments; currently unused.

what: Get ESPN MLB Season Power Index (Long Format) — espn_mlb_powerindex
title: Get ESPN MLB Season Power Index (Long Format) — espn_mlb_powerindex
text: Returns ESPN's power-index related per-team metrics one MLB season, long format: one row per (team x stat). Auto-paginates teams. Backed sports.core.api.espn.com/v2/sports/baseball/leagues/mlb/seasons/{season}/powerindex. Note: ESPN's power-index feed sparsely populated MLB frequently returns zero items.

what: Get ESPN MLB Team-in-Season Profile — espn_mlb_team_season_profile
title: Get ESPN MLB Team-in-Season Profile — espn_mlb_team_season_profile
text: Era-correct team identity MLB franchise specific season, plus available $ref URLs deeper resources (record, statistics, leaders, coaches, etc.). Backed core-v2 endpoint sports.core.api.espn.com/v2/sports/baseball/leagues/mlb/seasons/{season}/teams/{team_id}. Historical depth goes back 1947 (MLB founding). Older seasons return fewer $ref keys; missing refs become NA.

Options "al", "nl", "". qual (character) Whether want batters/pitchers qualified given season, minimum number plate appearances inclusion. want qualified hitters, use qual. minimum number plate appearaces/innings pitched, use number desired. Defaults "y". startseason (character) Season want scrape data. endseason (character) Last season want data. startdate (character) Start date want data. enddate (character) End date want data. month (character) Month want data. hand (character) Handedness batter. Options "L", "R", "B". Empty string returns . team (character) Teams want data, comma separated. pageitems (character) Number items per page. pagenum (character) Page number. ind (character) Whether break seasons individual, roll together. 1 = split seasons, 0 = aggregate seasons. rost (character) Whether include players roster. 1 = include, 0 = exclude. players (character) Whether include players roster. 1 = include active roster players, 0 = exclude. type (character) Defaults 8, standard leaderboard. values leaderboards appear go type = 0 48+, correspond links leaderboard page. postseason (logical) Whether include postseason data. TRUE = include postseason, FALSE = exclude postseason. sortdir (character) Sort direction. Options "asc" "desc" "default". sortstat (character) Sort stat. Default "WAR".

title: (legacy) Scrape Pitcher Leaderboards from FanGraphs — fg_pitch_leaders
text: age (integer) Age players pos (character) Position players, defaults "". exclude pitchers, use "np". stats (character) Statistic return. Defaults "bat". lg (character) League return. Defaults "". Options "al", "nl", "". qual (character) Whether want batters/pitchers qualified given season, minimum number plate appearances inclusion. want qualified hitters, use qual. minimum number plate appearaces/innings pitched, use number desired. Defaults "y". startseason (character) Season want scrape data. endseason (character) Last season want data. startdate (character) Start date want data. enddate (character) End date want data. month (character) Month want data. hand (character) Handedness batter. Options "L", "R", "B". Empty string returns . team (character) Teams want data, comma separated. pageitems (character) Number items per page. pagenum (character) Page number. ind (character) Whether break seasons individual, roll together. 1 = split seasons, 0 = aggregate seasons. rost (character) Whether include players roster. 1 = include, 0 = exclude. players (character) Whether include players roster. 1 = include active roster players, 0 = exclude. type (character) Defaults 8, standard leaderboard. values leaderboards appear go type = 0 48+, correspond links leaderboard page. postseason (logical) Whether include postseason data. TRUE = include postseason, FALSE = exclude postseason. sortdir (character) Sort direction. Options "asc" "desc" "default". sortstat (character) Sort stat. Default "WAR".

what: Calculate FIP and related metrics for any set of data — fip_plus
title: Calculate FIP and related metrics for any set of data — fip_plus
text: function allows calculate FIP related metrics given set data, provided right variables data set. function currently returns FIP per inning pitched, wOBA (based batters faced), wOBA per instance fair contact.

title: Calculate FIP and related metrics for any set of data — fip_plus
Calculate FIP and related metrics for any set of data — fip_plus
text: df data frame statistics includes, minimum, following columns: IP (innings pitched), BF (batters faced), uBB (unintentional walks), HBP (Hit Pitch), x1B (singles), x2B (doubles), x3B (triples), HR (home runs), AB (-bats), SH (sacrifice hits), (strike outs), season.

code: ggspraychart(   data,   x_value = "hc_x",   y_value = "-hc_y",   fill_value = NULL,   fill_palette = NULL,   fill_legend_title = NULL,   density = FALSE,   bin_size = 15,   point_alpha = 0.75,   point_size = 2,   frame = NULL )

title: Generate spray charts with ggplot2 — ggspraychart
text: data data frame includes batted ball coordinates. Typically, coordinates come GameDay xml feed downloads baseballsavant.com x_value x coordindate. Typically hc_x. y_value y coordinate. Typically hc_y. generally need inverse negative hc_y values, recommended calculate plotting. fill_value categorical variable want geom_points base fill . Pass string. left blank, defaults blue. fill_palette object containing customer palette used ggplot2::scale_fill_manual. fill_legend_title string containing custom legend title used ggplot2::scale_fill_manual. density Chooses 2d density plot point plot. Defaults FALSE. bin_size Size bins used building density plot. Defaults 15. point_alpha Alpha value whenever geom_point used. Defaults .75. Recommend .3 density plots. remove points density points set use point_alpha = 0. point_size Set size geom_point used. frame Variable use frame argument using gganimate create animated plots. density plots sure variable factor.

title: MLB Attendance — mlb_attendance
text: team_id Return attendance information particular team_id(s). league_id Return attendance information particular league_id(s). Format: '103,104' season Return attendance information particular year(s). date Return attendance information particular date. Format: MM/DD/YYYY league_list_id Unique league list identifier return directory attendance specific league list_id Valid values include: milb_full milb_short milb_complex milb_all milb_all_nomex milb_all_domestic milb_noncomp milb_noncomp_nomex milb_domcomp milb_intcomp win_noabl win_caribbean win_all abl mlb mlb_hist mlb_milb mlb_milb_hist mlb_milb_win baseball_all

title: MLB Standings — mlb_standings
text: season Year(s) return return standings information . date Date return return standings information . standings_type standings_type(s) return standings information . Description standings_types regularSeason - Regular Season Standings wildCard - Wild card standings divisionLeaders - Division Leader standings wildCardWithLeaders - Wild card standings Division Leaders firstHalf - First half standings.  valid leagues split season (Mexican League). secondHalf - Second half standings. valid leagues split season (Mexican League). springTraining - Spring Training Standings postseason - Postseason Standings byDivision - Standings Division byConference - Standings Conference byLeague - Standings League league_id league_id(s) return standings information .

title: MLB Stats — mlb_stats
text: MLB Stats

title: MLB Stats — mlb_stats
code: mlb_stats(   stat_type = NULL,   player_pool = NULL,   game_type = NULL,   team_id = NULL,   position = NULL,   stat_group = NULL,   season = NULL,   league_id = NULL,   sport_ids = NULL,   sort_stat = NULL,   order = NULL,   limit = 1000,   offset = NULL )

title: MLB Stats — mlb_stats
text: stat_type Stat type return statistics . player_pool 4 different types player pools return statistics particular player pool across sport. Acceptable values include: , Qualified, Rookies, Qualified_rookies game_type Game type return information particular statistic particular game type. team_id Team ID return information ranking particular statistic particular team. position Position return statistics given position. Default "Qualified" player pool Acceptable values include: P C 1B 2B 3B SS LF CF RF DH PH PR BR SP RP CP UT UI UO RHP LHP RHS LHS LHR RHR B X stat_group Stat group return information ranking particular statistic particular group. season Year return information ranking particular statistic given year. league_id League ID return statistics given league. Default "Qualified" player pool. sport_ids sport_id(s) return information ranking information . sort_stat Sort return based stat. order Order return based either desc asc. limit limit limit return particular number records. offset offset returns +1 first record set players.

title: MLB Stats Leaders — mlb_stats_leaders
code: mlb_stats_leaders(   leader_categories = NULL,   player_pool = NULL,   leader_game_types = NULL,   sit_codes = NULL,   position = NULL,   stat_group = NULL,   season = NULL,   league_id = NULL,   sport_id = NULL,   start_date = NULL,   end_date = NULL,   stat_type = NULL,   limit = 1000 )

title: MLB Stats Leaders — mlb_stats_leaders
text: leader_categories League leader category return information ranking particular statistic. player_pool 4 different types player pools return statistics particular player pool across sport. Acceptable values include: , Qualified, Rookies, Qualified_rookies leader_game_types Game type return information ranking particular statistic particular game type. sit_codes Situation code return information ranking particular statistic particular game type. position Position return statistics given position. Default "Qualified" player pool Acceptable values include: P C 1B 2B 3B SS LF CF RF DH PH PR BR SP RP CP UT UI UO RHP LHP RHS LHS LHR RHR B X stat_group Stat group return information ranking particular statistic particular group. season Year return information ranking particular statistic given year. league_id League ID return statistics given league. Default "Qualified" player pool. sport_id sport_id return information ranking information . start_date Start date return information ranking particular statistic particular date range. Format: MM/DD/YYYY start_date must coupled end_date byDateRange stat_type end_date End date return information ranking particular statistic particular date range. Format: MM/DD/YYYY end_date must coupled start_date byDateRange stat_type stat_type stat_type return information ranking particular statistic particular stat type. limit limit limit return particular number records.

title: Query Baseball Savant Leaderboards — statcast_leaderboards
text: function allows read leaderboard data BaseballSavant directly R data frame.

title: Query Baseball Savant Leaderboards — statcast_leaderboards
code: statcast_leaderboards(   leaderboard = "exit_velocity_barrels",   year = 2020,   abs = 50,   min_pa = "q",   min_pitches = 100,   min_throws = 100,   min_field = "q",   min_run = 0,   player_type = "batter",   fielding_type = "player",   oaa_position = "",   oaa_roles = "",   team = "",   arsenal_type = "n_",   run_type = "raw",   min2b = 5,   min3b = 0,   position = "",   bats = "",   hand = "" )

title: Query Baseball Savant Leaderboards — statcast_leaderboards
text: leaderboard type leaderboard retrieve, input string. Current options include exit_velocity_barrels, expected_statistics, pitch_arsenal, outs_above_average, directional_oaa, catch_probability, pop_time, sprint_speed, running_splits_90_ft, arm_strength. year season want data. abs minimum number batted balls. Applies exit_velocity_barrels leaderboards. min_pa Minimum number plate appearances. Can number 'q' qualified batters. min_pitches Minimum number pitches thrown. min_throws Minimum number throwing opportunities. min_field Minimum number fieding opportunities. min_run Minimum number running opportunities. player_type One either 'batter' pitcher. expected_statistics leaderboard, 'batter-team' 'pitcher-team' also available. fielding_type One either 'player' 'team'. oaa_position Can either number position player '' '' position categories. oaa_roles Can either number position player '' '' position categories. team abbreviation team. Can left blank. arsenal_type One either 'n_', 'avg_spin', 'avg_speed'. run_type One either 'percent' 'raw'. min2b minimum number throwing attempts second base. min3b minimum number throwing attempts third base. position numeric position player. DH use 10. Can left blank. bats handedness batter. One 'R' 'L'. Can left blank. hand handedness pitcher. One 'R' 'L'. Can left blank.

title: Query Baseball Savant Leaderboards — statcast_leaderboards
text: Returns tibble Statcast leaderboard data following columns (leaderboard: 'exit_velocity_barrels'):

title: Query Statcast by Date Range and Players — statcast_search
code: statcast_search(   start_date = Sys.Date() - 1,   end_date = Sys.Date(),   playerid = NULL,   player_type = "batter",   ... )  statcast_search.default(   start_date = Sys.Date() - 1,   end_date = Sys.Date(),   playerid = NULL,   player_type = "batter",   ... )  statcast_search_batters(start_date, end_date, batterid = NULL, ...)  statcast_search_pitchers(start_date, end_date, pitcherid = NULL, ...)

title: Query Statcast by Date Range and Players — statcast_search
text: start_date Date first game want data. Format must YYYY-MM-DD format. end_date Date last game want data. Format must YYYY-MM-DD format. playerid MLBAM ID player whose data want query. player_type player type. Can batter pitcher. Default batter ... currently ignored batterid MLBAM ID batter whose data want query. pitcherid MLBAM ID pitcher whose data want query.

title: Query Statcast by Date Range and Players — statcast_search
text: Returns tibble Statcast data following columns: Returns tibble Statcast data. Returns tibble Statcast pitch-level columns statcast_search(), filtered requested batter. See statcast_search() return value full column--column reference. Returns tibble Statcast pitch-level columns statcast_search(), filtered requested pitcher. title: baseballr 2.0.0
text: Game data (site.api.espn.com): espn_mlb_scoreboard(), espn_mlb_pbp() (pitch/-bat level), espn_mlb_team_box(), espn_mlb_player_box(), espn_mlb_game_all(), espn_mlb_game_rosters(), espn_mlb_teams(), espn_mlb_team_current_roster(), espn_mlb_standings(), espn_mlb_betting(). Baseball-specific extractors basketball analogue: espn_mlb_game_probables() (probable / announced starting pitchers) espn_mlb_game_info() (venue, attendance, game duration, umpire crew). Reference data (sports.core.api.espn.com): athletes, coaches, seasons, franchises, draft, futures, leaders, positions, powerindex, events, plays, per-competitor game detail, season_* family. Athlete data (site.web.api.espn.com): espn_mlb_player_overview(), espn_mlb_player_gamelog(), espn_mlb_player_splits(), espn_mlb_player_stats(), espn_mlb_team_stats(). Box scores returned wide, clean-named baseballr_data tibbles (one row per team espn_mlb_team_box(); one row per athlete-side, tagged stat_group, espn_mlb_player_box()), baseball batting/pitching/fielding stat groups parsed natively. ESPN requests share internal httr2 layer (.retry_request()) honours options(baseballr.proxy = ...). Live tests gated behind ESPN_MLB_TESTS=1 (skip_espn_test()).

title: baseballr 2.0.0
text: edge_frequency(df, group = ...) now groups column named group argument. previously grouped literal column named group (.data$group), passing group (e.g. "pitcher") errored unless data happened group column otherwise ignored argument. statcast_search() now assigns Baseball Savant’s columns length-tolerantly recognizes newest Savant column definitions, columns added (reordered ) CSV export longer break function “can’t assign N names M column data.table” error (#337, #354, #371, #390). sptrc_team_active_payroll() sptrc_league_payrolls() updated Spotrac’s new /payroll/_/year/<year>/ URLs changed table schema; parsing now resilient column-order changes functions return data (#392). Wrappers build result inside tryCatch() now initialize return value first, API error returns empty value cli message instead object '<var>' found error. mlb_game_timecodes() longer returns NULL; renamed single column literal name ".", newer R versions longer use coerced column, now renames position. mlb_homerun_derby() mlb_homerun_derby_bracket() return data ; column selections now wrapped dplyr::any_of() dropped num_batters column (longer MLB Stats API response) longer errors parse. NCAA functions (ncaa_*()) work . stats.ncaa.org moved behind Akamai, returns HTTP 403 “Access Denied” requests lacking modern browser headers; .ncaa_headers() now sends current client-hint / fetch-metadata headers (sec-ch-ua, Sec-Fetch-*, Upgrade-Insecure-Requests), ncaa_teams() / ncaa_team_player_stats() now request https. request_with_proxy() now sleeps 5 seconds every request (NCAA edge aggressively rate-limits / IP-bans) accepts proxy argument (URL string list httr2::req_proxy() args, defaulting getOption("baseballr.proxy")) route rotate requests proxy. (Verified: ncaa_roster(), ncaa_teams().) ncaa_teams() longer returns NA ids. stats.ncaa.org migrated team links legacy /team/{team_id}/{season_id} pair single modern /teams/{season_team_id} resource, previous str_extract("(\\d+)/(\\d+)") matched nothing team_id/season_id came back NA. function now extracts ids whichever url shape present (legacy rows populate team_id/season_id; modern rows populate new season_team_id column) id columns never -NA site flips formats. ncaa_team_player_stats() builds request correctly fails gracefully (#379). batting branch previously passed id twice never sent year_stat_category_id, return batting stats; branches now key /team/{team_id}/stats season id per-type stat-category id (batting_id / pitching_id) season-id lookup. stats endpoint currently gated behind Akamai’s bm-verify interstitial challenge (verified: URL ids correct work real browser, static request — even valid session cookies carried roster page — served challenge, data). challenged, function now emits informative cli warning returns empty tibble (via new internal .ncaa_is_interstitial() guard) instead silently scraping table-less page. fg_guts(), fg_park(), fg_park_hand() return data . FanGraphs replaced legacy ASP.NET grid (HTML id GutsBoard1_dg1_ctl00) modern .table-scroll data grid, hard-coded id selector matched nothing; now target current grid, use https://, fetch okhttp User-Agent () Cloudflare challenge doesn’t block . FanGraphs functions return data FanGraphs’ current Cloudflare posture. 2026-06-03 FanGraphs serves Cloudflare JS challenge (HTTP 403, cf-mitigated: challenge) every unrecognized client — including plain/library User-Agent previously worked — header/TLS tweak passes (requires JS runtime). challenge exempts okhttp client FanGraphs mobile app uses, FanGraphs requests now send User-Agent: okhttp/4.12.0 get HTTP 200 normal JSON. dedicated internal fg_api_call() helper (okhttp UA) carries leaders/team functions (fg_batter_leaders(), fg_pitcher_leaders(), fg_fielder_leaders(), fg_team_batter(), fg_team_pitcher(), fg_team_fielder()); game-log functions (fg_batter_game_logs(), fg_pitcher_game_logs(), fg_milb_batter_game_logs(), fg_milb_pitcher_game_logs()) .aspx scrapers send UA. shared mlb_api_call() keeps plain package UA MLB Stats API (ignores ). (#404, #402, #397, #389, #385, #384, #373, #369, #361, #358, #353, #349, #343; okhttp-UA approach PR #405.)

title: baseballr 2.0.0
text: Added description column @return tables currently-returning functions (matching wehoop/hoopR documentation style), regenerated live API responses columns, types, descriptions reflect current output.

title: baseballr 2.0.0
text: Migrated entire HTTP layer httr httr2 dropped httr dependency. httr::modify_url() -> httr2::url_modify_query(); MLB/FanGraphs helpers NCAA helpers (request_with_proxy() / check_status()) now use httr2::request() / req_retry() / req_perform() / resp_body_string() / resp_status(). request_with_proxy() gained proxy argument (URL string list [httr2::req_proxy()] args, defaulting getOption("baseballr.proxy")) routing NCAA requests proxy. Migrated package’s internal code magrittr pipe (%>%) native pipe (|>); Depends now R (>= 4.1.0). User-facing messages migrated cli package. Column selections drop known-transient columns now use dplyr::any_of() resilience upstream schema drift. Added project documentation community health files (CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, pull request template), refreshed _pkgdown.yml reference index, updated GitHub Actions workflows current (Node 20-compatible) action versions.

title: baseballr 1.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-01-16 Primarily focused parameter changes fg_*() functions allow flexible data retrieval. See details. parameters now follows leaderboard functions: age (integer) Age players pos (character) Position players, defaults “”. exclude pitchers, use “np”. stats (character) Statistic return. Defaults “bat”. lg (character) League return. Defaults “”. Options “al”, “nl”, “”. qual (character) Whether want batters/pitchers qualified given - season, minimum number plate appearances inclusion. want - qualified hitters, use qual. minimum number plate appearaces/innings - pitched, use number desired. Defaults “y”. startseason (character) Season want scrape data. endseason (character) Last season want data. startdate (character) Start date want data. enddate (character) End date want data. month (character) Month want data. hand (character) Handedness batter. Options “L”, “R”, “B”. Empty string - returns . team (character) Teams want data, comma separated. pageitems (character) Number items per page. pagenum (character) Page number. ind (character) Whether break seasons individual, roll together. 1 = split seasons, 0 = aggregate seasons. rost (character) Whether include players roster. 1 = include, 0 = - exclude. players (character) Whether include players roster. 1 = include active roster players, 0 = exclude. type (character) Defaults 8, standard leaderboard. values - leaderboards appear go type = 0 48+, correspond links - leaderboard page. postseason (logical) Whether include postseason data. TRUE = include postseason, FALSE = exclude postseason. sortdir (character) Sort direction. Options “asc” “desc” “default”. sortstat (character) Sort stat. Default “WAR”. change affects following functions: - fg_team_batter() - fg_team_pitcher() - fg_batter_leaders() - fg_pitcher_leaders()

title: baseballr 0.3.4 (2018-05-29)
text: run_expectancy_code() function formats Baseball Savant data users can generate run expectancy different base-count-base-states. also append data frame new variables necessary generating linear weights (see new function ). argument data frame downloaded Baseball Savant Columns created appended Baseball Savant data: final_pitch_game: whether pitch final one thrown game final_pitch_inning: whether pitch final one thrown inning final_pitch_at_bat: whether pitch final one thrown bat runs_scored_on_pitch: many runs scored result pitch bat_score_start_inning: score batting team beginning inning bat_score_end_inning: score batting team end inning bat_score_after: score batting team pitch thrown cum_runs_in_inning: many cumulative runs scored beginning inning pitch runs_to_end_inning: many runs scored result pitch end inning base_out_state count_base_out_state: specific combination base-outs count-base-outs pitch thrown avg_re: average run expectancy base-count-base-state next_avg_re: average run expectancy base-count-base-state results pitch change_re: change run expectancy result pitch re24: total change run expectancy end inning resulting pitch based change base-count-base-state plus number runs scored result pitch/bat Example: run_expectancy_table() functions works run_expectancy_code function work generating run expectancy tables automatically exported Global Environment Example: linear_weights_savant() function works tandem run_expectancy_code() generate linear weights offensive events Baseball Savant data properly formatted. Currently, function return linear weights average linear weights outs. apply scaling align league wOBA. Users can like, may added future version function. Example: used Baseball Savant data 2010-2015 compared linear weights generated baseballr Tom Tango using retrosheet data. baseballr’s weights generally little lower Tango generated, due number things, data source, code, etc., values appear reasonable enough reliable: also great contributions others ’ve added release: label_statcast_imputed_data() Ben Dilday contributes cool experimental function meant tag batted ball cases significant imputation may used generate Statcast values MLBAM, .e. launch_speed launch_angle. can read Ben’s function . fg_park() Sam Boysel updated park factors function now includes new columns added FanGraphs (5-year, 3-year, 1-year park factors) ensures column names correct

title: baseballr 0.3.3 (2017-05-08)
text: latest release baseballr package R includes number enhancement acquiring data Baseball Savant well minor grammatical clean documentation. Previous functions scrape_statcast_savant_batter scrape_statcast_savant_pitcher allowed acquistion data baseballsavant.com given player user-determined time frame. However, somewhat inefficient want acquire data players given time frame. Two new functions added, scrape_statcast_savant_batter_all scrape_statcast_savant_pitcher_all, allow user acquire data either pitchers hitters given time frame. functions take two arguments: start_date: first date user wants records returned end_date: final date user wants records returned Remember, baseballsavant.com’s csv download option allows 50,000 records single query. works roughly 10-12 days games. Longer time frames take longer download. Example: acquire data batters 2017-04-03 2017-04-10
code: > head(scrape_statcast_savant_batter_all('2017-04-03', '2017-04-10')) [1] "These data are from BaseballSevant and are property of MLB Advanced Media, L.P. All rights reserved." title: baseballr 0.3.2 (2017-09-12)
text: latest release baseballr package R includes number enhancements bug fixes. terms new functions, statline_from_statcast allows users take raw pitch--pitch data Statcast/PITCHf/x calculate aggregated, stat line-like output. Examples include count data number singles, doubles, etc., well rate metrics like Slugging wOBA swings contact. function two arguments: df: data frame includes pitch--pitch information. function assumes following columns present: events, description, game_date, type. base: base indicates denominator rate stats calculated. function defaults “swings”, can also choose use “contact” example using data week 2017-09-04. , want see statline hitters based swings: can also combine statline_from_statcast function loop create statlines multiple players . Example: calculate statlines batters contact games played 2017-09-04 2017-09-10:
code: test <- scrape_statcast_savant_batter_all("2017-09-04", "2017-09-10")  statline_from_statcast(test)  year swings batted_balls  X1B X2B X3B  HR swing_and_miss swinging_strike_percent    ba 1 2017  13790        10663 1129 352  37 259           3127                   0.227 0.129  obp   slg   ops  woba 1 0.129 0.216 0.345 0.144 test <- scrape_statcast_savant_batter_all("2017-09-04", "2017-09-10")  output <- data.frame()  for (i in c("Jose Ramirez", "J.D. Martinez", "Francisco Lindor", "Gary Sanchez", "Rhys Hoskins")) {   reduced_test <- test %>%     filter(player_name == i)   x <- statline_from_statcast(reduced_test, base = "contact")   x$player <- i   x <- x %>%     select(player, everything())   output <- rbind(output, x) %>%     arrange(desc(woba)) }  print(output, width = Inf)  # A tibble: 5 x 12             player  year batted_balls   X1B   X2B   X3B    HR    ba   obp   slg   ops  woba              <chr> <chr>        <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> 1    J.D. Martinez  2017           17     4     1     0     7 0.706 0.706 2.000 2.706 1.092 2     Gary Sanchez  2017           11     3     1     0     2 0.545 0.545 1.182 1.727 0.710 3 Francisco Lindor  2017           27     4     2     1     3 0.370 0.370 0.852 1.222 0.498 4     Rhys Hoskins  2017           14     2     1     0     2 0.357 0.357 0.857 1.214 0.495 5     Jose Ramirez  2017           16     0     0     0     3 0.188 0.188 0.750 0.938 0.370

title: baseballr 0.2.1 (2016-10-08)
text: Updates functions release: scrape_statcast_savant_batterscrape_statcast_savant_pitcher New functions release: code_barrel research team Major League Baseball Advanced Media developed way categorize batted balls average batting average .500 slugging 1.500. specific coding criteria can found comment #2 [] (http://tangotiger.com/index.php/site/comments/statcast-lab-barrels#2). Now, whenever user scrapes Statcast data using either scrape_statcast_savant_batter scrape_statcast_savant_pitcher functions results include column barrel, batted ball matches barrel criteria code 1, otherwise 0. Example: already Statcast data–say, database ’ve collecting–’ve also included simple function take dataframe code whether row contains barrel . need pass dataframe code_barrel.
code: > scrape_statcast_savant_batter(start_date = "2016-04-06", end_date = "2016-04-15", batterid = 621043) %>%  +     filter(type == "X") %>% +     filter(!is.na(barrel)) %>% +     select(player_name, game_date, hit_angle, hit_speed, barrel) %>% +     tail() [1] "Be patient, this may take a few seconds..." title: baseballr 0.2.0 (2016-08-25)
text: Functions added release: scrape_statcast_savant_batterscrape_statcast_savant_pitcherplayerid_lookup two savant functions allow user retrieve PITCHf/x Statcast data either specific batter pitcher [Baseball Savants’ Statcast Search] (https://baseballsavant.mlb.com/statcast_search). user needs provide start date, end date, batter pitcher’s MLBAMID. Example: Since savant functions require users pass valid MLBAMID, lookup function included leverages Chadwick public register. Users provide text string players string present last name returned. example user looking players last name “Seager”:
code: > scrape_statcast_savant_batter(start_date = "2016-04-06", end_date = "2016-04-15", batterid = 621043) %>%       filter(type == "X") %>%      select(3,7,54:56) %>%      tail() [1] "Be patient, this may take a few seconds..."
